findings.title: Missing error-path tests for eval-store (TC-020, TC-021) and page component tests (TC-016 through TC-019)
findings.issue: Task plan specified 6 test cases (TC-016 through TC-021) that do not exist. TC-020 should verify that storeEvalRun propagates DB update failures. TC-021 should verify KV failure resilience. TC-016-019 should verify scanColor and VERDICT_TO_STATUS mapping. The page.test.tsx file does not exist at all. Implementation is correct but lacks regression test coverage for the error paths and verdict mapping.
findings.suggestion: Add TC-020 (mock db.skill.update to reject, assert storeEvalRun throws) and TC-021 (mock KV put to reject, assert storeEvalRun resolves) to eval-store.test.ts. Create page.test.tsx with TC-016-019 for scanColor and VERDICT_TO_STATUS validation. These are non-blocking because the implementation logic is verifiably correct by code inspection.
findings.impact: Without TC-020, a future refactor could silently revert the awaited DB update to fire-and-forget without test failure. Without verdict mapping tests, scanColor changes could break chip rendering silently.

findings.title: scanColor ERROR maps to #9CA3AF instead of spec-specified #EF4444
findings.issue: AC-US2-06 specifies scanColor('ERROR') should return '#EF4444' (red). Implementation uses '#9CA3AF' (light gray). However, this color is consistently used across EvalScoreDisplay.tsx, EvalVerdictBadge.tsx, and page.tsx, suggesting a deliberate design choice to visually distinguish ERROR (gray = system failure) from FAIL (red = quality failure).
findings.suggestion: If the design intent is to differentiate ERROR from FAIL visually, update spec AC-US2-06 to match the implementation (#9CA3AF). If the spec is authoritative, change all three files to #EF4444. The current implementation is internally consistent.
findings.impact: Users see a gray chip instead of red for ERROR verdicts. This is arguably better UX (ERROR != bad quality, it means evaluation failed) but deviates from the written spec.
